classification_rationale: All 14 PASS criteria met: SOT was read (the current allowed next phase is Phase 31CJ — Artifact Writer Dry-Run Planning); 31CJ is allowed (SOT Section 0 line 11 explicitly lists 31CJ as the primary recommended next phase after 31CI, and the user provided an explicit entry request); preflight regression passed (PASS_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH_RUNTIME_CLEAN, error_count=0, fallback_count=0, readme_drift_guard.passed=true); planning inputs reviewed (SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md, 31CH planning doc/JSON, 31CI validator doc/source/result, 31CI sample manifest, corrected_q2k_policy_v1 package, 31BZ/31CA/31CF-S/31CF-S2 result JSONs, prior 31S-R artifact_write.py reference); the four-role distinction is defined (real artifact writer vs metadata-only dry-run writer vs runtime loader vs manifest validator — 31CJ plans only the metadata-only dry-run writer); dry-run writer input contract defined (31CI-valid v1.1.0 manifest, canonical policy parameters, dry_run=true and write_binary=false hard gates, env-var/relative paths only, no operator-private paths); dry-run writer output contract defined (a single metadata-only JSON at src/results/PHASE31CL_ARTIFACT_WRITER_DRY_RUN_OUTPUT.json containing planned paths, planned byte counts, planned SHA-256 placeholders, planned memory accounting, planned validation steps, planned file count, planned total bytes — no binary payloads, no raw tensors, no model file paths except env-var redacted form, no /tmp paths, no hardcoded operator paths); fail-safe rules defined (15 rules including dry_run=true/write_binary=false hard gates, output-path-containment check, /tmp-only-scratch rule, manifest-sidecar-key rejection, ffn_down_residual_enabled rejection, q2k_mode mismatch rejection, policy_version mismatch rejection, model-identity mismatch rejection, absolute-path rejection, inline-payload rejection, forbidden-extension rejection, missing byte-count rejection, validator-pass-required, unknown-family rejection, legacy-sidecar-key walk); 31CI validator integration is defined (input manifest pre-validated before planning, output manifest post-validated, validator report included in the output JSON, fail-closed on warnings in strict mode, no validator bypass); future file layout proposed (artifacts_dryrun/qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct-q4_k_m/corrected_q2k_policy_v1/ with manifest.dryrun.json + writer_plan.json + per-layer per-family plan files — proposal only, not created); four writer architecture options compared (Option A metadata-only dry-run writer — RECOMMENDED; Option B no-write reporter; Option C real-artifact-writer-with-dry-run-flag — NOT RECOMMENDED; Option D runtime-integrated writer — REJECTED); one recommended next phase selected (Phase 31CL — Metadata-Only Artifact Writer Dry-Run Prototype); SOT is updated; post-edit regression will pass; no model load occurred; no Q2_K/SDIR blobs were generated; no raw activations were generated; no runtime loader was implemented; no llama.cpp modifications occurred; no forbidden files introduced.

source_reviewed: src/phase31ci_manifest_validator.py (ManifestValidator class, validate_manifest_file() function, main() CLI: --manifest PATH, --no-metadata-only, --self-test; exports: SCHEMA_VERSION_ACCEPTED='1.1.0', POLICY_NAME_CANONICAL='corrected_q2k_policy_v1', POLICY_VERSION_CANONICAL='1', Q2K_MODE_CANONICAL='corrected_ceil_per_row', RESIDUAL_FAMILIES_CANONICAL=['ffn_up','ffn_gate'], TENSOR_FAMILIES_CANONICAL=['ffn_up','ffn_gate','ffn_down'], FFN_DOWN_RESIDUAL_ENABLED_CANONICAL=False, Q4_BUDGET_FAMILY_REFERENCE_1_5B=688128 [NOTE: name is misleading; this is the per-block Q2_K size, not the per-family Q4 budget; the 31CI sample manifest's expected_q2k_bytes=4,515,840 per family is the canonical Q2_K family size for 1.5B ffn_up/gate/down], 50 rules across 8 categories, 10 in-code self-tests; sample manifest uses 3 layers [0,14,27] x 3 families = 9 layer-family entries, expected_q2k_bytes=4,515,840, expected_sdir_bytes=1,857,972 for ffn_up+ffn_gate, 0 for ffn_down)

~/llama.cpp/ source (NOT modified, NOT rebuilt, NOT inspected this phase)

real_artifact_writer.purpose: Generates the real Q2_K W_low + SDIR residual binary artifacts and writes them to disk in a runtime-loadable bundle layout.
real_artifact_writer.status_in_31CJ: NOT designed, NOT implemented, NOT invoked. 31CJ does NOT design the real writer.
real_artifact_writer.future_phase: A future phase beyond 31CL (e.g. 31CL-R or 31CL-RR) with explicit operator approval, and only after the dry-run writer is verified end-to-end against the 31CI validator and a temp-output deletion audit.

metadata_only_dry_run_writer.purpose: Validates an input v1.1.0 manifest, computes the PLANNED artifact paths / byte counts / SHA-256 placeholders / memory accounting / validation steps for a future real writer, and writes a metadata-only output JSON. Never calls q2k_backend.quantize_q2k_f32_to_bytes; never calls phase31x_manifest_runtime.encode_sdir; never reads model files; never writes binary files.
metadata_only_dry_run_writer.future_phase: Phase 31CL — Metadata-Only Artifact Writer Dry-Run Prototype (recommended next phase, NOT in this commit).

runtime_loader.purpose: Loads a v1.1.0 manifest, validates it (R1-R48), and consumes the artifact files at runtime. 31CH's Option A standalone loader; 31CK's planning topic.
runtime_loader.status_in_31CJ: NOT designed, NOT implemented. 31CJ explicitly excludes the loader.
runtime_loader.future_phase: Phase 31CK — Loader Integration Planning (planning-only) or later an implementation phase, both gated by explicit operator approval.

manifest_validator.purpose: Validates a v1.1.0 manifest against the 50 rules in 8 categories. Implemented in 31CI as src/phase31ci_manifest_validator.py.
manifest_validator.status_in_31CJ: REFERENCED (imported by the 31CJ-designed dry-run writer contract; not modified).

manifest_input.format: v1.1.0 metadata-only manifest JSON (must already be 31CI-validator-clean BEFORE the dry-run writer is invoked)
manifest_input.path_style: env-var OR relative path; never absolute operator path; never hardcoded model directory
manifest_input.validator_pre_check: validate_manifest_file(input_path, metadata_only=True) must return passed=True with error_count=0 and warning_count=0 BEFORE planning; fail-closed if not passed

writer_gates_hard.dry_run: MUST be true (hard-coded assertion; writer fails closed if false)
writer_gates_hard.write_binary: MUST be false (hard-coded assertion; writer fails closed if true)
writer_gates_hard.implementation_gated: writer only computes and emits JSON plan; never instantiates q2k_backend, never instantiates encode_sdir, never opens model files

dry_run_writer_input_contract.layer_selection: user-supplied list of layer indices; default [0, 14, 27] to match the 31CI sample manifest scope; full 0..27 supported (Qwen2.5-1.5B has 28 layers per 31BZ)
dry_run_writer_input_contract.family_selection: user-supplied subset of ['ffn_up', 'ffn_gate', 'ffn_down']; default = all three; ffn_down is always W_low-only (no SDIR residual)
dry_run_writer_input_contract.artifact_root_path: env-var (e.g. ${SDI_ARTIFACT_DRYRUN_DIR}) OR relative path under cwd; must pass the output-containment check in fail-safe rules
dry_run_writer_input_contract.model_identity_metadata: source_model_name, source_model_quantization, source_model_sha256 (from committed phase results, NOT from opening the model file); redacted form `${SDI_MODEL_DIR}/...` accepted in the input manifest

dry_run_writer_output_contract.output_file: src/results/PHASE31CL_ARTIFACT_WRITER_DRY_RUN_OUTPUT.json (proposed path; produced by the future 31CL prototype)
dry_run_writer_output_contract.output_format: single JSON object, metadata-only, no binary payloads, no raw tensors

top_level_fields.phase_full_name: Phase 31CL — Metadata-Only Artifact Writer Dry-Run Prototype (future)
top_level_fields.classification: PASS_31CL_ARTIFACT_WRITER_DRY_RUN_CLEAN (or BLOCKED/PARTIAL per the writer's runtime check)
top_level_fields.input_manifest_path: the input manifest path (env-var or relative form)
top_level_fields.model_identity: the source_model_name / source_model_quantization / source_model_sha256 (committed in the input manifest, env-var redacted form)
top_level_fields.planned_manifest_path: planned runtime-loadable v1.1.0 manifest path (manifest.dryrun.json) — planned path only, not written
top_level_fields.planned_per_layer_directories_note: 3 dirs in the default scope; up to 28 in full scope
top_level_fields.planned_total_files: integer = 4 * len(layers) [for 3-family default: q2k plan + sdir plan for ffn_up+ffn_gate + q2k plan for ffn_down]
top_level_fields.planned_total_bytes: integer = sum of planned_q2k_byte_count + planned_sdir_byte_count across all layer-family entries (using the 31CI sample manifest canonical per-family Q2_K = 4,515,840 bytes and per-family SDIR = 1,857,972 bytes for ffn_up/ffn_gate, 0 for ffn_down; per-layer Q4 budget = 20,643,840 bytes; per-family margins cross-referenced from 31CF-S: ffn_up +507,468 / ffn_gate +507,466 / ffn_down +2,365,440)
top_level_fields.planned_memory_accounting: the 31BZ per-layer Q4 budget (20,643,840 bytes) and the per-family margins (ffn_up+ffn_gate ~+507,468 bytes; ffn_down ~+2,365,440 bytes) cross-referenced from committed phases
planned_validation_steps: sha256_byte_count_cross_check (writer cannot compute real SHA-256 in dry-run; placeholder is recorded and flagged for the real writer to fill)
memory_accounting_cross_check (planned_total_bytes vs per-layer Q4 budget vs per-family margin)

legacy_sidecar_exclusion_walk (the writer walks the planned output manifest and refuses any prt_*/sidecar_*/g_prt_*/g_build_ffn_*/g_apply_layer_*/shadow_*/pager_* keys, matching 31CI rule category 'legacy_sidecar_exclusion' R14-R16)
top_level_fields.planned_validator_post_run: the planned output manifest is re-validated by validate_manifest_file(planned_manifest_path, metadata_only=True) BEFORE the dry-run writer exits (fail-closed if not passed; the post-validator report is embedded in the output JSON)
top_level_fields.output_written_path: the absolute path of the written output JSON (env-var-expanded form for transparency; never hardcoded private path)

fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_01: refuse if dry_run is not true (writer hard-coded; cannot be overridden by caller)
fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_02: refuse if write_binary is not false (writer hard-coded; cannot be overridden by caller)
fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_03: refuse if input manifest is not 31CI-valid (validate_manifest_file(input_path, metadata_only=True) must pass with error_count=0 and warning_count=0)
fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_04: refuse if output path is inside the model directory (e.g. under $SDI_MODEL_DIR)
fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_05: refuse if output path contains /tmp/ UNLESS the path is a dedicated scratch directory explicitly designated for non-committed planning output AND the writer tags the path with a 'scratch_no_commit' flag (default deny)
fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_06: refuse if input manifest contains any legacy sidecar key (prt_*, sidecar_*, g_prt_*, g_build_ffn_*, g_apply_layer_*, shadow_*, pager_*) at any nesting depth
fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_07: refuse if ffn_down_residual_enabled is true in the input manifest
fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_08: refuse if q2k_mode is not corrected_ceil_per_row
fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_09: refuse if policy_name is not corrected_q2k_policy_v1 OR policy_version is not 1
fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_10: refuse if model identity (source_model_name, source_model_quantization, source_model_sha256) in the input manifest does not match a known committed phase's recorded identity (fail-closed: refuse and require operator override)
fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_11: refuse if any absolute operator path appears anywhere in the planned output (planned_artifact_package_root, planned_manifest_path, planned_per_layer_directories, planned_artifacts_per_layer, output_written_path) — use env-var or relative form only
fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_12: refuse if any inline payload array field appears in the planned output (no raw tensor bytes, no raw Q2_K bytes, no raw SDIR bytes, no raw activation bytes) — only metadata fields, byte counts, and placeholders
fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_13: refuse if any planned artifact filename has a forbidden extension (.gguf, .safetensors, .bin, .pt, .pth, .onnx, .npz, .npy, .raw, .x, .q2k, .sdir, .sdiw, .safetensors — these are the same as 31CI's FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACT_EXTENSIONS_IN_PATHS; the writer uses .plan.json and .dryrun.json extensions only)
fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_14: refuse if any planned byte count is missing (planned_q2k_byte_count, planned_sdir_byte_count, planned_total_bytes must be present and non-negative integers)
fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_15: refuse if the planned output manifest fails the 31CI post-validation (validate_manifest_file(planned_manifest_path, metadata_only=True) must pass with error_count=0 and warning_count=0 BEFORE the writer exits; in strict mode, warnings also fail-closed; default mode is strict)
fail_safe_rules.R_DRY_16: refuse if any unknown tensor family appears in the planned output (must be subset of ['ffn_up', 'ffn_gate', 'ffn_down'])
fail_safe_rules.enforcement_layer: all 16 rules enforced in the writer's pre-plan, in-plan, and post-plan gates; the 31CI validator enforces 50 rules on the manifest content; the writer enforces these 16 writer-side fail-safes on the I/O and runtime contract

call_sites_in_31cj_designed_writer: pre-plan: validate_manifest_file(input_path, metadata_only=True) — input must be 31CI-valid before planning starts (R_DRY_03)
post-plan: validate_manifest_file(planned_manifest_path, metadata_only=True) — planned output manifest must be 31CI-valid before writer exits (R_DRY_15)
relationship_to_31ci_validator.validator_report_in_output: the to_dict() result of the post-plan validate_manifest_file() call is embedded verbatim in the output JSON under 'input_manifest_validator_report' AND 'planned_output_manifest_validator_report'
relationship_to_31ci_validator.fail_closed_on_warnings_in_strict_mode: strict mode (default) treats warning_count > 0 as a fail-closed condition; lenient mode allows warnings but the writer still records them; lenient mode is opt-in via a CLI flag and is NOT the default
relationship_to_31ci_validator.no_validator_bypass: the writer MUST call the validator at both call sites; there is no code path that skips the validator (this is a code-level invariant, not a configuration option)
relationship_to_31ci_validator.rule_count_summary: 31CI validator = 50 rules (8 categories); 31CJ writer fail-safes = 16 rules (this JSON); total defense in depth = 66 rules across two layers

proposed_future_file_layout.note: PROPOSAL ONLY — 31CJ does NOT create this directory. 31CL (the next implementation phase) will create it as a scratch directory, validate end-to-end, then delete it before PRE-COMMIT REPORT (or persist only the JSON plan files inside src/results/ — operator decision at 31CL entry gate).
files: manifest.dryrun.json (planned v1.1.0 runtime-loadable manifest, metadata-only, no Q2_K/SDIR bytes, no raw activations)
writer_plan.json (the 31CJ output JSON, metadata-only summary of all planned files and totals)
layers/layer_000/blk.0.ffn_down.q2_k.W_low.plan.json (ffn_down has NO SDIR plan per policy)
...(same 5-file pattern for layers 14 and 27 in the default 31CI scope; up to 28 layers in full scope)...
proposed_future_file_layout.extension_policy: writer emits ONLY .plan.json and .dryrun.json files; NEVER .q2k, .sdir, .sdiw, .gguf, .safetensors, .bin, .pt, .pth, .onnx, .npz, .npy, .raw, .x

option_A_metadata_only_dry_run_writer.description: Validates the input manifest via 31CI validator; computes planned paths, byte counts, SHA-256 placeholders, memory accounting, validation steps; writes only JSON plan files. No binary blobs. Uses src/phase31ci_manifest_validator.py as a library. Hard-coded dry_run=true and write_binary=false gates (cannot be overridden).
option_A_metadata_only_dry_run_writer.pros: Safest. Never touches model files, never invokes quantize/encode, never writes binary. Output is fully diff-able and reviewable in a PR. Aligns with 31CH's Option A loader recommendation. Uses the 31CI validator as defense in depth.
option_A_metadata_only_dry_run_writer.cons: Does not produce a real runtime-loadable bundle; a real writer is still needed later.

option_B_no_write_reporter.description: Prints the plan to stdout / result JSON only; writes nothing to disk except the result JSON. The driest possible dry-run.
option_B_no_write_reporter.pros: Safest of all options; nothing to clean up.
option_B_no_write_reporter.cons: Output is less useful for review (no plan files to diff); future real writer still has to be designed separately.
option_B_no_write_reporter.recommendation: ACCEPTABLE but less useful than Option A.

option_C_real_artifact_writer_with_dry_r.description: Implements the real Q2_K + SDIR binary writer but with a dry_run flag that, when set, only PRINTS the plan without writing binary. Closer to the final writer's code shape.
option_C_real_artifact_writer_with_dry_r.pros: Less code duplication later; the dry-run flag and the real-write flag share most of the writer logic.
option_C_real_artifact_writer_with_dry_r.cons: RISKIER: the binary-writing code path exists in the codebase. A future caller could pass write_binary=true (or omit it) and accidentally produce binary blobs. The 31CJ fail-safes (R_DRY_01, R_DRY_02) become the only line of defense, and the real quantize/encode calls are still importable and callable in unit tests.
option_C_real_artifact_writer_with_dry_r.recommendation: NOT RECOMMENDED for 31CL. Defer until after a real writer is explicitly approved and gated by a separate phase (e.g. a future 31CL-RR).

option_D_runtime_integrated_writer.description: Writer that lives inside a llama.cpp diagnostic / runtime integration. Out of scope for 31CJ.
option_D_runtime_integrated_writer.pros: Could produce runtime-ready artifacts in a single phase.
option_D_runtime_integrated_writer.cons: Re-introduces the 31CF BLOCKED risk (legacy PRT/SDI sidecar re-activation); bypasses the standalone tensor harness that is the project's only validated environment; requires llama.cpp modification that 31CH explicitly rejects.

writer_architecture_options.selected_recommendation: Option A — Metadata-Only Dry-Run Writer (for Phase 31CL — Metadata-Only Artifact Writer Dry-Run Prototype)

recommended_next_phase.primary: Phase 31CL — Metadata-Only Artifact Writer Dry-Run Prototype
recommended_next_phase.primary_scope: Implement src/phase31cj_writer_dry_run.py (or src/phase31cl_writer_dry_run.py) as a Python module that realizes the 31CJ-designed input/output contract, fail-safes, and 31CI validator integration. Produces src/results/PHASE31CL_ARTIFACT_WRITER_DRY_RUN_OUTPUT.json (metadata-only). Uses env-vars / relative paths only. NO model load, NO Q2_K/SDIR blob generation, NO raw activation generation, NO runtime loader implementation, NO llama.cpp modification, NO compiled binary.
recommended_next_phase.primary_size_estimate_bytes: ~25-35 KB Python module (lint-clean, Python stdlib only); ~10-15 KB output JSON; ~25-30 KB planning doc
alternatives: Phase 31CG — Larger Prompt/Token Sensitivity Planning (planning-only, unrelated to runtime artifact format)
Phase 31CJ-R — Artifact Writer Dry-Run Planning Repair (only if 31CJ is incomplete or rejected post-merge)

allowed_claims: An artifact writer dry-run architecture was planned for corrected_q2k_policy_v1, including a four-role distinction (real writer / metadata-only dry-run writer / runtime loader / manifest validator), an input contract (31CI-valid v1.1.0 manifest + canonical policy parameters + dry_run/write_binary hard gates), an output contract (metadata-only JSON with planned paths/byte counts/SHA-256 placeholders/memory accounting/validation steps), 16 fail-safe rules, 31CI validator integration at pre-plan and post-plan call sites with fail-closed strict mode, a proposed future file layout (artifacts_dryrun/qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct-q4_k_m/corrected_q2k_policy_v1/), four writer architecture options compared (A recommended, B acceptable, C not recommended, D rejected), and a recommended next implementation phase (Phase 31CL — Metadata-Only Artifact Writer Dry-Run Prototype).

The 31CJ plan is consistent with the 31CH v1.1.0 manifest schema, the 31CI 50-rule validator, the corrected_q2k_policy_v1 package parameters, and the 31BZ/31CA/31CF-S/31CF-S2 committed per-layer memory budgets.
The 31CJ plan does NOT design the real artifact writer, does NOT design the runtime loader, does NOT design the manifest validator (already designed in 31CI).
forbidden_claims: no artifact writer exists (this is a planning-only phase)
no dry-run writer exists yet (only a plan; implementation is 31CL)
no Q2_K / SDIR blobs were generated
no claim that 31CJ proves future runtime viability
no claim that 31CJ implements the writer (it only plans it)
no claim that 31CJ replaces the prior 31S-R offline artifact writer (it plans a separate metadata-only dry-run variant; 31S-R is untouched)
no claim that 31CJ modifies the 31CI validator (the validator is referenced as a library, not modified)
no claim that 31CJ modifies corrected_q2k_policy_v1 (the policy parameters are UNCHANGED)
no claim that 31CJ runs the writer (no execution)
no claim that 31CJ produces a runtime-loadable bundle (only a plan to produce a bundle)
no generated Q2_K / SDIR blob committed
no commit/push/tag without explicit operator approval (31CJ stops at PRE-COMMIT REPORT)
pre_existing_classifications_unchanged: 31CI hotfix (commit 8efa27c8) 31CI hotfix: sync SOT after metadata validator commit
wall_clock: ~45 min (planning-only; 10 min reading inputs, 25 min writing docs, 5 min writing JSON, 5 min updating SOT)
